name: Data Layer Consolidation
acceptance_criteria_met: ✓ performance_data.py exists and is syntactically valid
✓ Each function returns a dict with documented keys
✓ Empty data sources don't crash (return data_points: 0)
summary: Successfully created unified performance data access layer with 7 standardized functions covering health metrics, tasks, content, and mood data. Fixed database schema compatibility issue with activity data column names. All functions tested and working with graceful error handling.

functions_implemented: get_weekly_sleep() - Returns sleep hours with daily breakdowns
get_weekly_tasks() - Returns completion metrics and pending counts
get_weekly_content() - Returns content library addition metrics
get_weekly_mood() - Returns mood entries from journal/bio snapshots
get_data_freshness() - Returns staleness status for all data sources
technical_details.trend_calculation: Compares current period vs previous period, ±5% = stable
technical_details.error_handling: Graceful fallbacks with data_points: 0 for missing sources

issues_encountered.issue: Database column name mismatch for activity data
issues_encountered.resolution: Updated query to use 'active_zone_minutes_total' instead of 'active_minutes'
issues_encountered.impact: Fixed activity function to work with actual schema

notes: Module is ready for integration by D1.4. All database access is read-only as required. Data freshness monitoring shows some sources (content/journal) are stale but this doesn't block functionality.
